recommendations.title: Issue a public health advisory for the affected zone
recommendations.action: Issue an advisory recommending that residents limit outdoor exertion and that sensitive groups remain indoors while concentrations stay elevated.
recommendations.reason:
An advisory is defensible on the measured concentration alone. It does not depend on knowing the source, which is why it survives even when the attribution evidence is weak or contested.

Scope: Does NOT justify emergency resource deployment.
assumptions: FIRMS detections are treated as a proxy for emissions. FRP measures radiated power, not smoke mass; the relationship is assumed monotonic, not calibrated.
The influence kernel (exponential distance decay, cosine upwind alignment, 24h linear recency) is a modelling choice. Results are sensitive to it.
Wind at the station is assumed representative of the transport path. In reality wind veers with height and distance over a 200 km trajectory.
Satellite overpasses are periodic. An absence of detections in the last hours may be an absence of overpass, not an absence of fire.
limitations: The advisory does not reduce emissions. It transfers risk management to residents.
This is evidence of fire influence, not a measure of fire contribution. No µg/m³ attributable to biomass is claimed here.
Cloud cover suppresses detection. During heavy winter haze the satellite may under-report exactly when the question matters most.
The kernel cannot distinguish stubble burning from other biomass (landfill fires, crop residue in other states, domestic burning).
recommendations.confidence_note: The strongest recommendation this engine makes, because it rests on a measured concentration rather than on an inferred source.
references: NASA FIRMS VIIRS 375m active fire product (VNP14IMG).
Kass, R. & Raftery, A. (1995). Bayes Factors. JASA 90(430).

decision_trace.detail: biomass evidence: moderate ★★★ (quality high, identification strong)

Moderate biomass evidence. Defensible because: Upwind fires are observed but the influence is not decisive. An advisory and closer observation are proportionate; committing the sprinkling fleet is not.
Issue a public health advisory for the affected zone

recommendations.title: Increase monitoring cadence at the affected station
recommendations.action: Raise the reporting cadence at this station and its neighbours, and flag the episode for review once the window closes.
recommendations.reason:
Always defensible: it commits no resources beyond observation and cannot be wrong in the way an intervention can. It is the correct response to uncertainty.

Scope: Does NOT justify emergency resource deployment.
assumptions: FIRMS detections are treated as a proxy for emissions. FRP measures radiated power, not smoke mass; the relationship is assumed monotonic, not calibrated.
The influence kernel (exponential distance decay, cosine upwind alignment, 24h linear recency) is a modelling choice. Results are sensitive to it.
Wind at the station is assumed representative of the transport path. In reality wind veers with height and distance over a 200 km trajectory.
Satellite overpasses are periodic. An absence of detections in the last hours may be an absence of overpass, not an absence of fire.
limitations: Does not reduce emissions or exposure.
This is evidence of fire influence, not a measure of fire contribution. No µg/m³ attributable to biomass is claimed here.
Cloud cover suppresses detection. During heavy winter haze the satellite may under-report exactly when the question matters most.
The kernel cannot distinguish stubble burning from other biomass (landfill fires, crop residue in other states, domestic burning).
recommendations.confidence_note: Carries no attribution claim. Recommending observation is never overreach.
references: NASA FIRMS VIIRS 375m active fire product (VNP14IMG).
Kass, R. & Raftery, A. (1995). Bayes Factors. JASA 90(430).

decision_trace.detail: biomass evidence: moderate ★★★ (quality high, identification strong)

Moderate biomass evidence. Defensible because: Upwind fires are observed but the influence is not decisive. An advisory and closer observation are proportionate; committing the sprinkling fleet is not.
Increase monitoring cadence at the affected station

assumptions: FIRMS detections are treated as a proxy for emissions. FRP measures radiated power, not smoke mass; the relationship is assumed monotonic, not calibrated.
The influence kernel (exponential distance decay, cosine upwind alignment, 24h linear recency) is a modelling choice. Results are sensitive to it.
Wind at the station is assumed representative of the transport path. In reality wind veers with height and distance over a 200 km trajectory.
Satellite overpasses are periodic. An absence of detections in the last hours may be an absence of overpass, not an absence of fire.

NO2 is treated as a traffic-responsive species. Supported by the COVID lockdown (NO2 -54.4% while SO2 held at -3.7%), but lockdown also stopped construction and industry, so the association is an upper bound.
SO2 is treated as dominated by point sources (power, industry) rather than traffic. Consistent with SO2 holding flat while traffic stopped.
The NO2/SO2 ratio is assumed to separate distributed from point-source combustion. Measured LR is 2.11 — weak. It leans, it does not decide.
Delhi's commute peaks are assumed at 07-11 and 17-21 IST.

SO2 is treated as a marker for point-source combustion (coal power, industry). Supported indirectly: SO2 held flat (-6.6%) through the COVID lockdown while NO2 halved (-54.4%), consistent with essential power generation continuing while distributed sources stopped.
No assumption is made about how much PM2.5 any industrial source contributes. No such estimate is possible from our data.
limitations: This system does not measure source contributions. Recommendations are derived from evidence for competing hypotheses, not from apportionment.
Recommendations are generated by deterministic rules. No model and no language model is involved, so the same evidence always yields the same advice.
The advisory does not reduce emissions. It transfers risk management to residents.
This is evidence of fire influence, not a measure of fire contribution. No µg/m³ attributable to biomass is claimed here.
Cloud cover suppresses detection. During heavy winter haze the satellite may under-report exactly when the question matters most.
The kernel cannot distinguish stubble burning from other biomass (landfill fires, crop residue in other states, domestic burning).
Does not reduce emissions or exposure.

contradicting_evidence.label: Hour 23:00 IST is outside commute peaks (07-11, 17-21)
SO2 11.0 µg/m³ is below 3x the Delhi median (11.6 µg/m³)
Industrial proximity not ingested — cannot check upwind sources

human_review_reasons: Data quality is poor. The evidence may be based on sparse or missing observations.

Historical validation is still pending for: Dedicated industrial intervention, Diwali 2019 (discriminant validity), Odd-Even II, April 2016, Stubble season vs non-season. Those modules have not yet survived a test that could reject them.
